headline: Rural Broadband: The Coverage Number That Counts Wrong
context: MCMC reports 97% broadband coverage nationally, but the metric counts any signal — including speeds too low for a video call — as covered.

cards.big: 97% coverage. 42% of rural users cannot load a webpage.
cards.sub: The definition of 'coverage' includes speeds under 1 Mbps.

cards.big: JENDELA target: 100% 4G by 2025. Actual: 82% as of Q4 2025.
cards.sub: Sabah and Sarawak interior remain largely on 3G or satellite.

cards.big: Five telcos received RM2.1B in coverage obligations. Penalties: zero.
cards.sub: USP contributions fund expansion. Compliance audits are not published.

cards.big: Starlink approvals stalled 18 months over equity conditions.
cards.sub: 30% Bumiputera equity requirement applied to a satellite service with no local infrastructure.

cards.big: Measuring signal instead of usable speed flatters the numbers.
cards.sub: Rural Malaysians know the difference between a bar of signal and a working connection.

cards.big: The gap is not technology. It is honest measurement and enforced obligations.
